title: Alphonsea lutea
intro: Alphonsea lutea, also known as the yellow Alphonsea, is a plant species in the family Annonaceae, native to tropical regions. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation and respiratory issues, due to its reported health benefits such as antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Alphonsea lutea include alkaloids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Alphonsea lutea is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as teas, decoctions, and extracts, often used to treat coughs, colds, and fever, as well as to aid in digestion and reduce inflammation.
